title: Threshold

protagonist_arc.lie_believed: The past holds nothing but pain. Forward motion is the only honest response to loss.
protagonist_arc.ghost: She was not at his bedside when he died. She was on stage.
protagonist_arc.want: Sell the house in 48 hours. Return to the city. Resume.
protagonist_arc.need: Stay still. Stay long enough to grieve. Find out who her father was when she wasn't watching.
protagonist_arc.truth_needed: Her father's silence was not indifference. He had a language she didn't speak.
protagonist_arc.arc_midpoint: The journal entry — Nadia's name in her father's handwriting, describing her piano playing with an emotion she had never attributed to him: longing.
protagonist_arc.arc_resolution: Nadia postpones the sale. She does not yet know why. The reader does.

beat_sequence.description: Nadia arrives at the house — clinical, purposeful, armored
The house is smaller than she remembered. The key still fits. The grief is still there.
In the study, she finds the journal. One entry. 1987.
In the garden at dusk, journal in hand. The phone call to the estate agent.
Not an answer. A stay of execution. The grief has a shape now.

thematic_controlling_idea: grief can only be completed by staying, not by leaving

motifs.description: Nadia carries the house key in her coat pocket for four months without opening the envelope it came in. The key appears in all three scenes.
Both father and daughter speak through objects rather than words. The journal breaks this pattern — Robert used words, privately, that he could not use aloud.
The house is smaller than Nadia remembered. Her father was smaller than his myth. The wound is smaller — and more specific — than grief in the abstract.

narrative_time.embedded_analepsis: The journal entry (1987) is read in the present. It functions as an embedded analepsis: the past speaks directly to the present without scene-level flashback.

focalization_strategy: third_limited throughout, fixed on Nadia. The father is never focalized — he exists only as object: the house, the study, the journal, Nadia's memories.
conformance_notes: This story uses 3 scenes across 2 chapters. It is designed as a complete short-story-length GBR example demonstrating: person_vs_self collision, embedded analepsis, flat arc for a non-present antagonist, and a redirected rather than achieved want outcome.
